## [00:00 - 03:06]

Hi, this is Don Crowder. Let's talk about the minimum viable webinar funnel. So just like there's a minimum viable webinar, there's also a minimum viable webinar funnel, the least amount of work to generate something that you can test and then improve on to get the sales that you need. So the challenge of a minimum viable webinar funnel is that everything you take out of this funnel hurts your sales. So as you've seen in the past, here's the fully developed funnel. Every single element in that funnel is designed to increase sales. But how do you do this in terms of the concept of the minimum viable webinar funnel without killing your sales? And so when looking at this, there are two areas that can contribute to that that you possibly could take out. So the first one is the concept of having the sales page promo for those that didn't buy that you could not do that piece, simply push them to the replay promo, send them out a couple of emails, but not sending them specifically to a sales page. What that means is that you don't have to go create another sales page and all the work associated with that. And so that's one that you possibly could lose out of this process at the beginning, and then add it in as you go along. The second one is this whole concept of what about the 70% those who don't sign up for the webinar. So you could remove the what happens if they don't sign up for the webinar and what happens if they don't attend the webinar. And just add that in later also after all people have been running webinars without these elements for years. But these absolutely help to increase your sales. So just remember that these elements I just talked about are elements are the elements that can double to five times your sales. So frankly, I wouldn't recommend cutting them. Ideally, I'd like you to build out the entire funnel. Ideally, I'd like you to build out the full fledged webinar. And this whole concept of the minimum viable, I'd prefer you didn't do it. But here's the thing, this is your webinar, you can do whatever you want. These are best practices, which means that they're the things that apply in most cases. You may look at them and say, but this doesn't apply in mind. And that's possible. You'll definitely want to test to see if you have a concern with any of the elements in this funnel or in the webinar itself. My recommendation is to do the very best you can put together all the

pieces that you can do, then run the webinar funnel, analyze it, tweak it, and repeat. And do that over and over again, until your webinar is performing exactly the way you want it to.
